sections.paragraph:
ἄγριος, ία, ον (Hom.+; loanw. in rabb.)

① pert. to being in a natural state or condition, wild, of plants in the open field (Diod S 5, 2, 4; Artem. 4, 57; Jos., Bell. 5, 437; PSI 816, 3 [II b.c.]) Hs 9, 26, 4. Of animals (so Diod S 4, 17, 4 ζῷα; 4, 17, 5 θηρία; Arrian, Ind. 11, 11; 13, 1; PSI 406, 42; 409, 18 [III b.c.]; BGU 1252, 4; as a rule LXX; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 139) 1 Cl 56:11f (Job 5:22f); μέλι ἄ. honey fr. wild bees (Iambl. Erot. p. 222, 16 μέλιτται ἄγριαι w. their μέλι; Cat. Cod. Astr. X 86b, 6 ἀγριομέλισσα.—Others think of a plant product; cp. Ps.-Aristot., Mirab. 19 ἐν Λυδίᾳ ἀπὸ τῶν δένδρων τὸ μέλι συλλέγεσθαι πολύ; Diod S 19, 94, 10 φύεται παρʼ αὐτοῖς [i.e. the Nabataeans] ἀπὸ τ. δένδρων μέλι πολὺ τὸ καλούμενον ἄγριον, ᾧ χρῶνται ποτῷ μεθʼ ὕδατος; Jos., Bell. 4, 468) Mt 3:4; Mk 1:6; GEb 13, 79. By fig. ext., of persons wild in appearance: of women in black w. flowing hair Hs 9, 9, 5; more completely ἄ. τῇ ἰδέᾳ of a shepherd 6, 2, 5.

② pert. to being untamed or running one’s own course, uncontrolled, fig. ext. of 1, of desires savage, fierce (Pla., Rep. 572b) Hm 12, 1, 2; cp. 12, 4, 6. τὸ ἄγριον cruelty (Pla., Rep. 571c et al.; Herm. Wr. 486, 38; 492, 4 Sc.) IEph 10:2 (opp. ἥμερος). Of natural phenomena stormy (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.) κύματα ἄ. θαλάσσης (Wsd 14:1; SibOr 3, 778) Jd 13.—DELG s.v. ἀγρός. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. fr. = from

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ps.-Aristot Ps.-Aristot , s. Aristot.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

GEb GEb = Gospel of the Ebionites—List 1

ext. ext. = extension

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 15
